displayTitle: Name the Question, Then Pick the Meter

beats.narration: You can't feel radiation. Every fact comes from an instrument — and at Level Two, choosing it is your call. Name the question first.

payload.title: NAME THE QUESTION
payload.sub: At Level II, choosing the instrument is your call.

beats.narration: Rate right now — survey meter. How much have I accumulated — dosimeter. Is something about to go wrong — the alarming ratemeter.

payload.heading: Name the question, then the instrument

beats.narration: Match the range. Scintillators prove areas clean but overload. G M tubes saturate in strong fields. The ion chamber stays linear — your workhorse.

payload.heading: Match the operating range to the task
payload.note: ION CHAMBER: LINEAR 2 mR/h → TENS OF R/h

beats.narration: On the device side, inspect the retrieval train daily: drive cable, connectors, guide tube, collimator. A kink or mismatched connector can strand the source.
If the crank's feel changes mid-retrieval — stop, hold position, and survey. Source-out until the meter proves otherwise.

payload.sub: Source-out until the meter proves otherwise.
